(P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府堺市中区上之1215', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Suearata-jinja}} [[JA:陶荒田神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11658473}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Suearata Shrine | 画像=[[Image:Suearata-jinja1.jpg|280px]]
Torii and haiden | 位置={{Coord|34|30|37|N|135|31|21|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Suearata Shrine}} | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakai|Q193428|WD=Q193428|1=Sakai}}{{ill|lt=Naka-ku (Sakai)|Q1042487|WD=Q1042487|lt=Naka-ku|1=Naka-ku, Sakai}} Ueno 1215 | 祭神= {{ill|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto|1=Takamimusubi}}
Tsurugine-no-Mikoto
{{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Yae-Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}-kō | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 創建=around 90 BCE | 本殿={{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Suearata Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Suearata Shrine''' (すえあらたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Ueno, {{ill|lt=Naka-ku (Sakai)|Q1042487|WD=Q1042487|lt=Naka-ku|1=Naka-ku, Sakai}}, {{ill|lt=Sakai|Q193428|WD=Q193428|1=Sakai}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, and a Shikinaisha of {{ill|lt=Ōtori District|Q3270651|WD=Q3270651|1=Ōtori district}}, {{ill|lt=Izumi Province|Q1047117|WD=Q1047117|1=Izumi Province}}. It stands in the birthplace area of {{ill|lt=Sue ware|Q7634351|WD=Q7634351|1=Sue pottery}} (the ancestor of Japanese pottery), and is also called '''Tōki Ōmiya'''. It is famous as the Ebisu of pottery, and the January Hatsu-Ebisu festival draws many people from technical, manufacturing, and commercial fields. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto|1=Takamimusubi}}, Tsurugine-no-Mikoto, {{ill|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|WD=Q8009611|lt=Yae-Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}-kō * Sessha ** Ebisu Hall: “Yae-Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto” ** Benten-sha: “{{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}}” ** Yamada Shrine: “Ikutama-yorihime-no-Mikoto” ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōtataneko|1=UNKNOWN}}-no-Mikoto) ** Ōta Shrine: “Ōtatamiko-no-Mikoto” (mother of Ōtataneko) ** Tama-no-o Shrine: “{{ill|lt=Amenominakanushi-no-Kami|Q2842779|WD=Q2842779|lt=Ame-no-Mi-naka-nushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi}}” === Origin === In Sujin 8 (90 BCE), {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} chose {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōtataneko|1=UNKNOWN}} living in the Ōta forest of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sue Village|1=UNKNOWN}} as chief priest. This shrine was founded to enshrine his ancestral spirits, and is considered to have been founded around Sujin 8. At that time, a major epidemic spread and the land was devastated. One night, deity {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|1=Ōmononushi}} appeared in Emperor Sujin’s dream and said that if Ōtataneko were appointed priest and worship offered, the disease would be calmed. The emperor found Ōtataneko in {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Chinu Agata|1=UNKNOWN}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sue Village|1=UNKNOWN}} and appointed him priest of {{ill|lt=Mount Miwa|Q860627|WD=Q860627|1=Mount Miwa}} in Nara to worship Ōmononushi. The shrine name is said to derive from “{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Areta no Atai|1=UNKNOWN}},” who lived in the Ōta forest of the same Sue Village. Areta no Atai is said to have been a direct descendant of Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto, one of the enshrined deities. {{-}} === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sue Village|1=UNKNOWN}} ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ueno|1=UNKNOWN}} - Ōaza Ueno, {{ill|lt=Higashi-Tōki Village|Q11528668|WD=Q11528668|1=Higashitōki}}, {{ill|lt=Ōtori District|Q3270651|WD=Q3270651|1=Ōtori district}} ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tōki-Kita|1=UNKNOWN}} - Ōaza Kitamura, Higashi-Tōki Village, Ōtori District. Site of {{ill|lt=Tōki Domain|Q10863472|WD=Q10863472|1=Tōki Domain}}. ** {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tsujino|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Tazono (Sakai)|UNKNOWN|lt=Tazono}} - Ōaza Tsujino and Tazono, {{ill|lt=Nishi-Tōki Village|Q11629968|WD=Q11629968|1=Nishitōki}}, Ōtori District. {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Suearata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Suearata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Suearata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Supershrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Sakai, Osaka]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:土器]] [[Category:Pottery]] [[Category:History of Naka Ward, Sakai City]] [[Category:堺市中区の建築物]]
